analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: Every GEMM in the graph runs on the FP32 SIMT path with the Tensor Cores completely idle. The two logit projections [8192,512]x[512,32000] alone consume 85.4% of all attributed GPU time (10.70 ms of 12.53 ms). The dispatched kernel is 'cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_256x128_8x4_tn_align1' -- the literal SIMT single-precision GEMM, not a Tensor-Core (HMMA/IMMA) kernel. The capture also reports that Inductor warned TF32 was disabled, so cuBLAS/CUTLASS had no FP32-via-TF32 Tensor-Core path available and fell back to FP32 ALU MACs.

transformation.description: Enable the TF32 Tensor-Core math mode for FP32 GEMMs. This keeps FP32 storage/semantics for activations and weights (no graph dtype change, no accuracy cliff for an inference logit head) but lets CUTLASS/cuBLAS select the TF32 Tensor-Core kernel instead of the SIMT sgemm path. The 10-bit-mantissa TF32 multiply runs on the Tensor Cores at multiples of SIMT FP32 throughput, directly attacking the tensor_core_active_pct=0.0 / sm_throughput=63.7% compute bottleneck on the two dominant logit GEMMs. This is a lowering-policy decision Inductor owns, so it is applied as an inductor_config / backend flag rather than a graph-node rewrite.
transformation.location: Backend entry point, before compile_fx is invoked (global math-mode flag honored by the GEMM lowering).
fx_steps: # Applied as a backend config flag, not FX node surgery.
# Inside the custom backend, before compile_fx(gm, example_inputs):
transformation.code_hint: torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True  (set once at backend init; routes cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_* -> TF32 Tensor-Core kernel)

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: None (storage stays FP32; gain is purely arithmetic throughput from engaging Tensor Cores)

optimizations.notes: Lowest-risk highest-leverage change: it touches all six GEMMs (100% of attributed compute) with no graph mutation and no register_buffer, and directly resolves Inductor's own TF32-disabled warning. Impact estimate is conservative (~2x on the SIMT-bound GEMMs); duration_ns is ncu-inflated so this is a relative figure only. If bf16 (OPT-2) is adopted, OPT-1 becomes redundant for those nodes but remains the safe fallback if bf16 accuracy is rejected.

bottleneck.description: The two logit GEMMs are simultaneously compute-bound on the SIMT FP32 path (Tensor Cores idle) AND carry a very large FP32 output write. Each [8192,512]x[512,32000] produces an [8192,32000] FP32 logit tensor = ~1.05 GB written to DRAM (dram write bytes ~991 MB per kernel in the profile). bf16 promotion attacks both axes at once: it routes the GEMM to the full bf16 Tensor-Core (HMMA) path (higher peak than TF32) and halves the 1 GB logit write to ~0.5 GB.

transformation.description: Insert bf16 casts around the decomposed GEMM primitives so activation and weight operands enter aten.mm/aten.addmm as bf16, with FP32 accumulation. The matcher walks the post-AOTAutograd graph for aten.mm.default / aten.addmm.default nodes, inserts an aten._to_copy (dtype=bf16) before each matmul input and an aten._to_copy back to fp32 on the result feeding downstream fp32 consumers (or leaves the logit output bf16 if the consumer is argmax/loss). This is an aten-level rewrite because it operates on the decomposed primitives and their dtypes, not the high-level module.
transformation.location: Post-AOTAutograd aten graph; rewrite each aten.mm.default and aten.addmm.default node and its operand edges.
fx_steps: if node.op == 'call_function' and node.target in MM_TARGETS:
mat_args = node.args[-2:]  # the two matrix operands (addmm: bias is args[0])
if a in mat_args and isinstance(a, torch.fx.Node):
back.args = (node,)  # restore self-edge severed by replace_all_uses_with
transformation.code_hint: Wrap aten.mm/aten.addmm operands in aten._to_copy(dtype=bfloat16); keep FP32 accumulate; optionally leave the [8192,32000] logit output in bf16 to bank the 0.5 GB write saving.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: ~0.5 GB per logit GEMM saved on output write (FP32 1.05 GB -> bf16 0.52 GB), ~1 GB total across the two logit heads; plus halved weight-read traffic on every GEMM.

optimizations.notes: Supersedes OPT-1 on the nodes it touches (bf16 HMMA peak exceeds TF32). Confidence is medium rather than high because bf16 changes numerics on a 32000-way logit head; validate top-k / argmax agreement against the FP32 baseline before adopting. Per the standing buffer rule, any pass that calls register_buffer (OPT-3 pre-transpose) must run after this dtype promotion so the frozen weight is materialized at bf16 -- that ordering is automatically satisfied because OPT-3 is inductor_config (a later funnel level than this aten pass).

bottleneck.description: The selected GEMM kernel is the 'align1' variant ('cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_256x128_8x4_tn_align1'). align1 means CUTLASS could prove only 1-element alignment on the operands and therefore disabled vectorized (128-bit) global loads and the wider, aligned Tensor-Core tiles. Combined with FP32 (OPT-1) or bf16 (OPT-2), giving the constant weight a frozen, properly-aligned/pre-transposed layout lets the GEMM dispatcher pick an align8 Tensor-Core kernel with coalesced vector loads instead of the scalar align1 fallback. Achieved occupancy is only 16.66% and registers_per_thread is 212 on this kernel; the aligned Tensor-Core templates use a different (lower) register footprint.

transformation.description: Enable Inductor weight freezing so the constant projection weights are constant-folded and re-laid-out (pre-transposed to the 'tn'-preferred contiguous layout and padded to a Tensor-Core-friendly alignment). With frozen, aligned constant weights the GEMM autotuner/dispatcher can select an align8 Tensor-Core kernel rather than the align1 SIMT fallback, improving global-load vectorization and tile efficiency. This is a constant-weight layout/lowering decision Inductor already owns, hence ir_level=inductor_config keyed on the 'freezing' config switch.
transformation.location: Backend entry point: set Inductor config before compile_fx; eval/inference graph only (freezing requires no-grad).
fx_steps: from torch._inductor import config as inductor_config
# freezing constant-folds and re-lays-out constant weights; requires inference (no autograd) graph
# max_autotune_gemm lets Inductor benchmark aligned Tensor-Core GEMM templates
# against the align1 fallback and pick the faster aligned kernel.
transformation.code_hint: inductor_config.freezing = True + max_autotune_gemm = True; lets the dispatcher replace cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_*_align1 with an aligned Tensor-Core template.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Improved global-load vectorization (align1 -> align8) raises effective L1/L2 bandwidth utilization on weight reads; no net byte reduction.

optimizations.notes: Incremental on top of OPT-1/OPT-2 (it improves the kernel selection that those passes enable). Must run after OPT-2 because freezing materializes the constant weight buffer at the runtime dtype -- satisfied automatically by level ordering (inductor_config is the last funnel stage, after the aten dtype-promotion pass). Requires a true inference/eval graph (no grad); freezing is a no-op or errors under training. Impact estimate is the most uncertain of the three (depends on whether the autotuner finds a strictly-better aligned template), hence medium confidence and lowest priority.

global_notes: 2 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined 0.66% of attributed time): aten::addmm fused gelu (0.43%) and aten::embedding fused native_layer_norm (0.23%).

hardware-limits.md was not present in the repository; architecture context derived from the device string 'NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition' (Blackwell / GB202-class, ~188 SMs). Per Blackwell guidance, warp_cycles_per_instruction is unavailable (confirmed null for every operator) and latency-bound diagnosis uses eligible_cycles_pct < 20; the dominant GEMMs show eligible_cycles_pct ~64.5%, so they are NOT latency-bound -- they are compute-bound on the SIMT FP32 path.

MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were unavailable; per the fallback policy, FX API patterns were drawn from built-in knowledge and novel/uncertain transforms are capped at medium confidence. knowledge/fx-patterns.md was also absent.

fused_kernel_double_count flag set: aten::embedding (fused_with native_layer_norm) and aten::addmm (fused_with view+gelu) are is_fused=true Inductor Triton kernels; their aten-level fused_with members are not separately timed, so do not double-count them. Both fall below the 1% threshold and are not targeted.
duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection overhead and are used here only for RELATIVE comparison within this profile, never as absolute latency.

capture_metadata contains no measure_iters field, so warm_up_inflation could not be evaluated; note that the operator graph is a 2-block stack (op11 consumes op7's output via input_op_ids=(7,0)), i.e. two sequential MLP+logit blocks, not two profiling iterations. The two logit heads are therefore data-dependent and cannot be naively batched into a single GEMM.

Root cause across the whole graph is identical: FP32 storage with TF32 disabled forces every matmul onto the cutlass_80_simt_sgemm SIMT path with tensor_core_active_pct=0.0. The three proposals are an escalating ladder addressing this single mechanism: OPT-1 (TF32, safe) < OPT-2 (bf16, faster + memory win, needs accuracy check) < OPT-3 (frozen aligned weights, kernel-selection polish). Adopt OPT-1 unconditionally; prefer OPT-2 if bf16 accuracy is acceptable; layer OPT-3 on whichever is chosen.
